---
title: "Network Science: Structure, Dynamics, and Inference"
subtitle: "Track C — Agent-Based Modeling"
date: "2025-01-01"
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
number-sections: true
---

# Network Science: Structure, Dynamics, and Inference

**Track**: Track C — Agent-Based Modeling

The mathematical theory of networks: random graphs, small-world and scale-free structures, community detection, dynamics on and of networks, and network inference from data.

## Table of Contents

1. Graph Theory Fundamentals
2. Random Graph Models
3. Small-World and Scale-Free Networks
4. Community Detection
5. Centrality and Network Structure
6. Dynamics on Networks: Epidemics and Diffusion
7. Temporal and Multilayer Networks
8. Network Inference and Reconstruction

---

## Chapter 1: Graph Theory Fundamentals

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 2: Random Graph Models

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 3: Small-World and Scale-Free Networks

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 4: Community Detection

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 5: Centrality and Network Structure

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 6: Dynamics on Networks: Epidemics and Diffusion

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 7: Temporal and Multilayer Networks

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*

## Chapter 8: Network Inference and Reconstruction

*This chapter has not yet been generated. Run the generation pipeline to produce full content.*
